name: domain-context
title: Domain Context & Architecture Decision Records
purpose: Capability: defines CONTEXT.md (domain glossary) and docs/adr/NNNN-slug.md (Architecture Decision Records) as advisory ADV-consumed domain artifacts. These artifacts capture durable domain knowledge and design decisions; ADV gates MAY read and write them per the scenarios below. Both requirements are `should`-priority advisory artifacts — they don't gate-block any phase. Adoption is opt-in across projects.

requirements.title: CONTEXT.md Domain Glossary Format and Consumers
requirements.body: Projects MAY maintain a `CONTEXT.md` at the repo root (single-context) or a `CONTEXT-MAP.md` pointing to per-context `CONTEXT.md` files (multi-context). The format records project-specific domain terms with their canonical definitions, aliases to avoid, relationships, example dialogue, and flagged ambiguities. `/adv-discover` MAY read CONTEXT.md during current-state analysis to align on domain language. `/adv-design` MAY append terms when new vocabulary is resolved during design. `/adv-clarify` MAY read CONTEXT.md to align clarification with established domain language. Lazy creation only — no script auto-scaffolds CONTEXT.md.

scenarios.title: CONTEXT.md present and readable by discover
given: A repo contains a CONTEXT.md at the root with at least one Language section term
then: The discover output MAY reference terms defined in CONTEXT.md
The discover output MUST NOT contradict CONTEXT.md term definitions without surfacing the conflict

scenarios.title: CONTEXT.md absence does not block discovery
given: A repo does not contain a CONTEXT.md
then: No automatic scaffolding of CONTEXT.md occurs

scenarios.title: CONTEXT-MAP.md identifies multi-context repos
given: A repo contains a CONTEXT-MAP.md at the root listing multiple per-context CONTEXT.md files
then: The map is read first to identify which context applies
Per-context CONTEXT.md files are read for context-specific vocabulary

requirements.title: Architecture Decision Records Format and Sparingly Rubric
requirements.body: Projects MAY maintain `docs/adr/NNNN-slug.md` Architecture Decision Records for durable design decisions. ADRs are sparse by design — only decisions meeting all three rubric criteria SHOULD be recorded: (1) hard to reverse (the cost of changing your mind later is meaningful); (2) surprising without context (a future reader will wonder "why did they do it this way?"); (3) result of a real trade-off (there were genuine alternatives and you picked one for specific reasons). `/adv-design` MAY emit ADR drafts during Phase 2/3 when the rubric is met. The minimum ADR is title + 1-3 sentences (context + decision + rationale). Optional sections (Status, Considered Options, Consequences) included only when they add value. Lazy creation only — no script auto-scaffolds docs/adr/.

scenarios.title: ADR rubric criteria all met
given: A design decision is hard to reverse, surprising without context, and result of a real trade-off
then: An ADR draft MAY be emitted to docs/adr/NNNN-slug.md
The draft includes the 3-criteria check showing all met

scenarios.title: ADR rubric criteria not all met
given: A design decision is easy to reverse OR obvious without context OR has no real alternatives
then: No ADR is drafted for that decision
The decision MAY still appear in design.md Key Decisions

scenarios.title: ADR numbering is sequential
given: docs/adr/ contains zero or more existing ADRs
then: The new ADR uses the highest existing number plus one
The slug summarizes the decision in 3-5 hyphenated words

scenarios.title: ADR absence does not block design
given: A repo does not contain docs/adr/
then: No automatic scaffolding of docs/adr/ occurs unless an ADR is being written
